mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, consumer spending power deteriorates rapidly, causing equity markets to price in recessionary risks and produce negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with 60-day forward returns showing negative or zero alpha in > 60% of co-occurring periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive equity returns over the next month during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during easing cycles, with a negative Sharpe ratio on the signal over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns exhibit no positive correlation with payroll growth, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 220 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that erode real corporate margins and prompt market repricing, yielding negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, invalidating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales expansion confirm broad-based demand resilience, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal generates negative 20-day returns in > 50% of aligned observations, or interaction term coefficients are statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs demand factors, causing negative 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with a positive mean return exceeding 0.5% in those periods.
